name: Executive Inbox - Backstory Starter
description: Automates executive email triage by reading unread email messages, identifying those from customers or prospects, enriching them with CRM context from Backstory, and using AI to classify and route each message. The AI Agent analyzes the email content alongside account history to determine urgency, category (support escalation, deal progression, renewal, executive outreach, etc.), and the appropriate internal channel or person. Routed messages land in the right Messaging channel (Slack, Teams, or Email) or trigger follow-up workflows, ensuring nothing falls through the cracks.

nodes.name: Workflow Parameters
nodes.notes: Fetch unread external emails from the executive inbox and include sender, subject, body preview, and thread metadata. Replace the placeholder API endpoints and tokens with real systems before enabling this workflow.

nodes.name: Get Backstory Auth Token
nodes.notes: Optional if you are using MCP only, but kept here to match the existing repo’s n8n patterns.

nodes.name: Fetch Source Records
nodes.notes: Fetch unread external emails from the executive inbox and include sender, subject, body preview, and thread metadata.

nodes.name: Normalize Source Records
nodes.notes: Converts raw executive inbox messages responses into one item per record for downstream AI analysis.

nodes.name: Analyze Executive Inbox

parameters.text:
=You are generating the Executive Inbox workflow output.

PURPOSE:
Automates executive email triage by reading unread email messages, identifying those from customers or prospects, enriching them with CRM context from Backstory, and using AI to classify and route each message. The AI Agent analyzes the email content alongside account history to determine urgency, category (support escalation, deal progression, renewal, executive outreach, etc.), and the appropriate internal channel or person. Routed messages land in the right Messaging channel (Slack, Teams, or Email) or trigger follow-up workflows, ensuring nothing falls through the cracks.

SOURCE PAYLOAD:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.record, null, 2) }}

TASKS:
1. Use Backstory MCP to enrich the record with the most relevant account, opportunity, engagement, or stakeholder context.
2. Decide what matters most for the executive inbox use case.
3. Produce a concise markdown summary with: headline, key findings, risks, recommended actions, and routing notes if applicable.

OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS:
- Keep it compact enough for Slack.
- Prefer bullets.
- Mention concrete dates, owners, amounts, or stages when the data provides them.
- If the source record is incomplete, explicitly say what is missing.

REFERENCE STYLE:
📬 **Executive Inbox Triage** — 6 emails classified | 2 urgent

🔴 **URGENT — Immediate Action:**
- **From:** Dan Reeves, VP Engineering @ ACME Corp
- **Subject:** "Need to discuss contract terms before Friday"
- **Account:** ===$425,000=== deal in Negotiation stage
- **Context:** Dan is the economic buyer — first direct email in 3 weeks. Backstory shows 2 missed calls from Dan yesterday
- 👉 Routed to: #acme-deal-room + @sarah.chen (account owner)

- **From:** Lisa Wong, Director of IT @ Globex Industries

options.systemMessage: You are a Backstory workflow analyst generating Executive Inbox outputs. Keep the response actionable, concrete, and Slack-friendly.

nodes.notes: Wire your preferred LLM credentials to the chat model node after import.

nodes.name: Backstory MCP
nodes.notes: This mirrors the existing workflow infrastructure in the repo. Add MCP auth headers after import.

nodes.name: Aggregate Results
nodes.notes: Collapses all analyzed records into a single outbound summary. Replace this with per-owner routing if you want one message per rep/CSM/executive.

nodes.name: Post to Slack
nodes.notes: Swap this node for Teams or SMTP if Slack is not your delivery target.
